basics.name: Andrew G. Grossman, MSc, MBA
basics.summary: Seasoned developer and engineering lead with 20+ years of experience, including a background in commercial data collection and processing. Strong background in PostgreSQL, Python, and Django, with experience in AWS, GCP, Kubernetes, ETL, optimization, and software development management.

work.name: Bestow
work.summary: Staff Software Engineer on the Enrollment Platform team from February 2025; promoted to Engineering Manager of the Enrollment Platform Core team in January 2026.
highlights: Built the first vendor data integration in a new framework, then directed and trained team members through two subsequent integrations delivering APS and EHR data to underwriters.
Enabled multi-actor insurance applications (owner ≠ insured) through cross-team coordination, delivering juvenile products for Transamerica and USAA including a live indexed universal life product.
Scoped a release tooling initiative to stabilize the team's deployment process and drove execution through three Central Europe contractors.
Served as hiring manager for two teammates (one contractor, one FTE) and onboarding buddy for two others as the broader division scaled from ~10 to 30+ engineers.
Built hands-on proficiency in GCP, Kubernetes, New Relic, Pub/Sub, Protobufs, and gRPC; introductory exposure to Go and Drools.

work.name: Cabinet Health
work.summary: Backend engineer for Cabinet's online pharmacy offering. Implemented software features in a Python/Django-based system supporting the customer account portal, internal business management systems, and integrations with pharmacy partners.
highlights: Designed and optimized API endpoints to handle 10M+ provider and pharmacy records for prescription acquisition, reducing refill response times by 30%. Cleaned underlying third-party data using Polars.
Implemented an automated notification system to send timely SMS and email alerts, increasing refill rates by 20% and optimizing operational workload by 15%.

work.name: Songtrust / Downtown Music Publishing
work.summary: Engineering lead for the Songtrust product team. Responsible for the development and maintenance of the Songtrust.com web application with 30k+ active users, as well as internal facing enterprise tools. Managed a team of 6 engineers.
highlights: Identified $35K/year of since-realized savings in cloud costs.

Functioned as departmental service owner for Postgres. Managed AWS RDS Postgres instances, including right-sizing, cost management, major version upgrades, and parameter configuration. Mentored new departmental DBA.
Completed initiative to verify user identities, reactivating stalled business partnership impacting $2M of client earnings.
Implemented SLA for incoming support requests, ensuring 95% of requests are responded to within 24 hours. Cleared 9 month request backlog in 3 months.
Applied more comprehensive monitoring and alerting, identifying ~75% of user-facing issues before they were reported.

work.name: Songtrust / Downtown Music Publishing
work.summary: Team lead / back-end developer for a greenfield application managing music industry intellectual properties for the purpose of royalty collection.  Managed and mentored 9 engineers across multiple development teams.
highlights: Architected data model, including support for robust auditability.
Designed and implemented a custom ETL pipeline using Python and SQL for data ingress from multiple systems, including MS SQL Server sourced data.
Maintained application deployment environments (AWS EC2/RDS Postgres/S3).
Drove approach to reduce data record rejections from business partners by 95% versus the previous system.

work.name: Songtrust / Downtown Music Publishing
work.summary: Full-stack developer on a consumer-facing Django/PostgreSQL/Ubuntu/AWS-hosted consumer-facing application for facilitating songwriter royalties collection.

work.name: Notes Frequencies, Inc.
work.summary: Backend architect for Notes' financial management platform for musicians. Automated and exposed data retrieval activities to service Notes' client-facing app and internal tooling.
highlights: Architected greenfield application based on Django/Django-Ninja/PostgreSQL/Celery/Docker/AWS.
Automated processing and loading of financial statements using Pandas.

work.name: Bridgestone Commercial Solutions
work.summary: Led a team of of 8 developers to build a commercial data collection product, that served as a key differentiator in the retread service package offering to hundreds of B2B customers. Product was built using a Django/Dojo/PostgreSQL/RHEL stack. At time of departure, the application had over 600 users and had collected over 3 million survey readings.
highlights: Architected and implemented application foundation using Django/Tastypie/Dojo frameworks.
Engineered third-party data imports (ETL) from various formats into PostgreSQL database.

work.name: Bridgestone Commercial Solutions
work.summary: Developed and administered Django-based commercial website for the tracking of national trucking fleet inventories. Resulting website is now marketed directly to large trucking fleets including Waste Management, Ryder, and UPS, leading to increased sales of the company's core product. Product was built using a Django/YUI 2/PostgreSQL/RHEL stack. System consolidated data from over 30 Postgres-based systems into a single reporting database. Worked on other software products as needed.

prunedWork.name: Stifel Nicolaus
prunedWork.summary: Developed advanced Microsoft Excel applications w/VBA for stock selection and tracking.

education.area: Master of Science in Computer Science
courses: Completed research paper in algorithm development including simulation and statistical analysis entitled "Indirect Data Reporting in Wireless Sensor Networks with Multiple Mobile Nodes."

education.area: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
